biological.physical_capacity: 5'8", lean functional build from three years of martial arts cross-training (muay thai, wrestling, jiu-jitsu). Moves economically — the kind of physical presence that registers with peers without being performed. Puberty accelerating through the summer.

attachment.forming_context: Karen is reliably present and warm, but her own anxiety transmits through her body language. Drew learns that love is dependable but slightly nervous.

active.name: emotional_inhibition
active.core_belief: Strong feelings make things worse — keep it together
active.compensatory_strategy: Channels emotion into productivity and problem-solving. Reads rooms before speaking. Defaults to 'I'm fine.' Marcus and Carol are partial exceptions — Drew is honest about thinking with them, though not about feeling. First crack: wrote a letter to Sofia rather than filing the feeling away. Still channeling through a structured medium rather than direct expression, but for the first time the feeling drove the action rather than being managed into silence.
active.forming_context: Greg models stoic competence. Karen's anxiety spikes when Drew is upset. Plano culture rewards cheerful compliance.
active.healing_note: Age 14 — chose to act on a feeling rather than suppress it. Not healing yet, but the schema's hold on relational expression is no longer absolute.

active.name: self_sacrifice
active.core_belief: My job is to make things easier for the people around me
active.compensatory_strategy: Volunteers for tasks, anticipates what others need, measures his own worth by usefulness. Now extending to the friendship with Marcus — Drew sometimes defers to Marcus's project ideas without examining whether he wants something different. First real test: wrote the Sofia letter for no reason except wanting to. Not useful to anyone, not solving a problem. The schema was present as a background pull — 'this doesn't help anyone' — and he overrode it.
active.forming_context: Post-9/11 household anxiety, Greg's job instability, Karen stretched thin. Drew decided to be the kid who doesn't add to the burden.

tested_by.context: Wrote a letter to Sofia Reyes — first act of self-direction in the relational/emotional domain. Not driven by competence or usefulness, but by want. Self-direction expanded from intellectual and physical domains into desire itself.

values.last_reordered: age 13 — self-direction moved to top as Drew's competence domains (martial arts, computers, social navigation) gave him increasing confidence in his own judgment

self_concept.agency: strong and expanding — three years of martial arts competition, real software projects, and social fluidity across groups built a robust sense of capability. Now extending beyond competence into intention: not just 'I figure things out' but 'I want things and I go after them.' The shift is early and fragile but present.
self_concept.communion: selective but genuine — deep bonds with Marcus and Carol, broad-but-shallow connections across peer groups, comfort with being between rather than belonging to. For the first time, communion is being approached not as a byproduct of usefulness but as something wanted in itself.
self_concept.dominant_sequence: emerging redemption arc — reactive excellence (ages 11-13) giving way to proactive intention. The realization that preparation without desire is just aimlessness. Not yet consolidated, but the first beat is laid.
self_concept.identity_statement: I am someone who figures things out, connects across boundaries, and prepares for what's coming. I don't need to belong to one group to belong. And I'm beginning to learn that wanting something — not because it's useful, not because someone else needs it, just because I want it — is also something I can be.

defenses.note: Anticipation added as a mature defense — Drew's preparation orientation (martial arts, systems thinking, social observation) is fundamentally about anticipating and preparing for challenges before they arrive. Defense profile is solidly mature under normal stress. Threshold raised slightly after the Sofia letter: Drew demonstrated he can tolerate the vulnerability of acting on a feeling without collapsing into self-protection. The question is what happens when stress exceeds the threshold — eighth grade's social volatility may test this more concretely than anything so far.
